Alright, we're back. Let's start this lesson with a quick recap of the five core elements of the straight line system. Number one is the product. You must absolutely love the product at your first ten. Number two, they must trust and connect with you, which is your second ten. Number three, they must trust and connect with the company that stands behind the product. Not your third ten, right? Number four is the action threshold. If necessary, you must lower the prospect's action threshold. And now lastly, we now come to the straight line's fifth core element, which is actually the key for closing those tougher sales so you can close anyone who's closable and it's called the pain threshold. Essentially, pain is that invisible force that drives people to take action. See, when someone's in massive pain, they'll take action even if they're not that certain about all three of the tens. In fact, people will do almost anything if they're feeling extreme pain in the moment to get out of that pain. They'll take pretty much any action they believe will get them out of that pain and feeling pleasure again. Here's a perfect example. It's a great story. You're gonna love this one. Now, like most people, I have always hated going to the dentist and it was this movie back then, it was like 85 or 86, Little Shop Horrors with Steve Martin played this vicious dentist like he was a masochist, right? Or a satist actually. Anyway, emphasis then was even worse, right? Because he's like just hysterical but terrible, right? Anyway, I had this problem with this tooth for a bunch of years. It was a cavity turned into a root canal and another root canal. They just couldn't fix this damn tooth, right? Anyway, finally, the dentist tells me he's got to yank the tooth out, right? And give me an implant. You know, one of those screws, right? It's gonna be a four or $5,000 he says, but it's gonna last me a lifetime, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, right? So I'm fine, let's do it. Now, at the time I was like 15 years sober, so I'm like, I got some free gas out of the deal. So I'm like, okay, I'll do it, get a free high from the dentist, a legal high, right? Anyway, he turns on the gas, does the implant, it's like a four month process now, because he's got to keep letting first the screws got to heal, the tissues got to heal, and on and on, I got to go back and forth, right? And as I'm going through this, he keeps giving me these temporary crowns made of porcelain to fill the space. But these damn temporaries, they keep cracking and then re-cracking because where it's situated in my mouth and I'm going crazy with this stuff, right? I have to keep running back and forth to this damn dentist and every meal ate to screw his stick on and I'm just going crazy, right? Total pay in the butt. Anyway, I finally get to the point where I'm so pissed off, I say to the guy, listen, can't you just make me a crown that's like will never crack? Like the industrial strength super duper crown, like I want to add up like the same titanium that you use on the space shuttle, right? It's impossible to break. He's like, well, yeah, I could do that. I got to get it made. I said, just get the thing made. Okay, what a cost? Boom. He goes out, gets it made, puts it in, fits like a glove and bam, that's that. So now I have to screw my mouth, right? And it's an awesome temporary, right? It's covered in, you know, white or looks cool, right? It's beautiful, gleaming like a diamond, built to last, right? But I still have to go back to this dentist to get the real implant, right? Which is another thousand bucks or so because remember, I have this thing over a screw. It's still in there, right? But let me ask you a question. I have this awesome temporary, fits perfectly. You think I ever went back to this dentist to get the permanent and put on? No, I kept this temporary in there for years. You know why? No pain, zero, not an ounce of discomfort, no uncomfortability at all. So from my perspective, why would I ever go back to the dentist? I hate the dentist, right? He wants to pull my old temporary out while it's still working fine, right? So from my point of view, why should I go back? So I didn't. Anyway, so years and years go by, here's what a story really gets funny. I mean, like, you know, five years later, and this thing is really built to last, right? And finally, my partner, Anne, who was my girlfriend back then, you know, Anne recommends, which in her case is actually giving me an order and you get it, right? So I don't have to listen to that, I feel even more pain without listening. That's the real pain in the stars, right? So she orders me, she says, it's time to take care of your health. You got to get this tooth taken out and get the real one, you know, she's a good woman, you know, all aspects of my health, right? She makes an appointment, she finds a great local dentist, I walk in there, beautiful, upscale, right? Sit down in the chair, he looks in, he's like, mm-hmm, mm-hmm, take some x-rays, and he goes, ah, yeah, yeah, I see, yeah, I see where the implant is, and, um, okay, yeah, oh, wow, interesting. They says to me, he goes, do you know what kind of glue they used? I'm like, what? He goes, well, you know, that thing is really good, it's really in there, you know what type of solvent, because if I don't know what type of solvent, I'm going to really have to hack that thing out, and I'm like, good lord, like, I'm thinking of Steve Morton, little shopper, come on, go and sit in the chair, and I imagine what that's going to feel like, I mean, he's actually, these guys, listen, he's actually future-pacing my own pain, right in front of me, and I'm thinking maybe I'll get lucky here, and he'll put the right solvent in and just dissolve, right, and then Dr. Death plows on, he says, all right, well, here it is, $2,900, and we'll do this in two or three sittings, maybe a third or one, a fourth one, and just to readjust the tooth, what do you think? Guess what I said? I said, sounds good, let me think about it, to which he replies, okay, great, let me know when you've decided, I'll schedule you in, and that was that, and I walked out, I go home, and I'm going to think about it, not, I'm not going to think about it, I'm never going back to this guy ever again, you know why? Because he did the absolute worst thing that a salesperson or influence could ever do, that includes a doctor or dentist or anybody else, any professional, he said to me, it's going to hurt you right now when I pulled this thing out, meaning he was going to put me in short-term pain, so when I'm future-pacing the idea myself, and I ask that magic question that every buyer asks themselves, namely, what's the worst that can possibly happen, in this case, if I leave this thing in, and my movie says, nothing really, it all seems okay, great temporary, titanium built to last, and my gut says, it'll probably stay in there forever, so screw it, I will wait until what? I'll wait until it hurts, then I'll go back and get it taken care of, that's what I'm going to do, you get it? However, let's now rerun this scenario a different way, what if this guy had understood even the slightest bit of the straight line system, what if he would have said to me, listen Jordan, I understand the temporary seems to be working well right now, but let me just tell you a few of the problems you might have over the long term, if you don't take this thing out, and by the way, I'm sure there are three or four things he could have said to me that were perfectly ethical, perfectly legit, right, because there are, in fact, there are lots of reasons why I should have had the thing taken out, it was a temporary for crying out loud, right, not a permanent, he could have said, you know, Jordan, you travel all over the world, you know, just imagine yourself being in Zambia or Zimbabwe, and all of a sudden the tooth finally cracks, it gives way, what do you do then Jordan, have some witch doctor put a new tooth in, just imagine the discomfort then, I mean, all this guy had to do was future pace me that much, you get it, he could have said, you're Jordan, this is going to bring a big problem for you, I've seen it happen many times before, it's a painful experience, let's just get this thing done right now, believe me, you'll be glad you did, sound good, and I'd be like, yeah, here's my credit card, and right on the spot, I would have done it without hesitation, but instead, I walked out with this business con, an estimate for a few thousand dollars from someone I would never, ever call again, makes sense, right, but how about this, let me give you even a different scenario, let's just say that I got home two weeks later, I bite into something and all of a sudden, bam, I get a pain, the tooth cracks, I'm in mortal pain, what are you gonna be doing then, I'll be running to the dentist and get it done right away, right, yes, of course I would, see if I feel pain, I act, and if any prospects feels pain, they act, if they don't feel pain, they will only act if you present them with something that fits just right, and that my friend is called your pain threshold, which is your fifth and final element of the straight line system, namely, how much pain you're currently feeling right now in the moment, and will this product help me resolve it, will it alleviate my pain, and with that, it's now quiz time, this is a crucial one here, so please take time to go through your quiz, and when you're done, I will see you in the next lesson, and we'll plow on, I'll see you there.
